body.title: Add importance flag to Envelope + receiver-selectable notification threshold
body.result: 35 bus tests green. importance field on Envelope, notified column on messages, notify_threshold on mailboxes. NOTIFY always fires (device wake); notified column for UI filter. Commit d1bfc122eeab84dede09020f799c311b6e9eecc1.
body.intention: I intend that every envelope carries an importance level (0-10) and that personal feed owners are notified only when incoming importance meets their configured threshold.
body.description:
Add importance flag to Envelope and wire notification gating into PgBus. Envelope gains importance: int = 3 (0=trace, 3=normal, 7=significant, 10=urgent). PgBus stores importance in bus.messages. A mailbox-level threshold (default 5) is stored in bus.mailboxes; PgBus.append() sets a notified flag only when importance >= threshold. Notification delivery mechanism is a LISTEN/NOTIFY on the existing pg_bus channel — the flag enables callers to filter before waking.

**Affected files:** bus/envelope.py, bus/pg_bus.py, schema migration for bus.mailboxes.notify_threshold + bus.messages.importance
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres only
**Scope boundary:** IN: importance field, threshold stored per-mailbox, notification gating at append. OUT: actual push notification delivery to web UI, device-level threshold configuration UI.
**Completion criteria:** (1) Envelope.importance: int = 3; (2) bus.mailboxes has notify_threshold int default 5; (3) PgBus.append() only fires NOTIFY when importance >= mailbox threshold; (4) PgBus.create_mailbox() accepts notify_threshold kwarg; (5) tests cover threshold gating.
